article_meta.title: By Jonah Goldberg July 11, 2016 11:14 AM PT Share via Close extra sharing options Email Facebook X LinkedIn Threads Redd

reasoning: No attribution violations and the flagged passages are genuine author-prose bias, but the model used non-listed bias types and missed several clear biased phrases in the author’s voice.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text is from the author’s prose, not quotes or attributed statements.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged item reflects defensible bias in the author’s language (framing of liberals, overgeneralization, pejorative toward Giuliani).
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed notable instances: 'National conversations' as efforts to 'bully everyone'; 'lazy certainty out of almost everybody'; 'that's true... and fairly irrelevant'; 'Liberals... are often rather obtuse'; 'nothing undermines... more than vilifying those sworn to uphold it.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used non-standard/unsupported labels (Loaded Language, Straw Man/Overgeneralization) instead of the defined set (e.g., Word Choice, Slant, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives, Mudslinging).
